# BetBro — Gold-Standard Voice Samples
*Generated by brand-voice skill — 2026-03-30*

These sentences represent the voice at its most authentic.
Use for reference, training, or comparison when output feels off.

---

## Landing page — Homepage hero
"RESEARCH SMARTER. BET OR WALK."
*Why it's representative: Six words that nail the entire brand — direct, confident, and the "walk" part is what makes BetBro different from every other tool.*

## Landing page — Features section
"THREE THINGS. THAT'S IT."
*Why it's representative: Fragment structure, anti-complexity stance, playful simplicity. This is how BetBro talks — no extra words.*

## Landing page — Value proposition
"WE'LL TELL YOU WHEN TO PASS."
*Why it's representative: The honesty differentiator in one line. Most tools only tell you what to bet — BetBro tells you what to skip.*

## Landing page — Anti-complexity positioning
"No charts. No guesswork."
*Why it's representative: Two fragments that position against overcomplicated competitors. Direct, confident, zero fluff.*

## Landing page — Discipline messaging
"Knowing when NOT to bet is the most underrated skill in sports betting."
*Why it's representative: Provocative — challenges the volume-first mentality. This is BetBro being the friend who tells you what you don't want to hear.*

## Landing page — Product description
"BetBro pulls the prop line, recent averages, injury reports, and matchup data — then gives you a plain-English breakdown."
*Why it's representative: Lists what it does without overselling. Ends with the accessibility promise — "plain-English breakdown."*

## Landing page — Coin-flip detection
"BetBro detects coin-flip scenarios and tells you this one's not worth it."
*Why it's representative: Shows the walk-away feature in action. Casual phrasing ("this one's not worth it") matches the buddy voice.*

## Landing page — Builder credibility
"Built by bettors, for bettors."
*Why it's representative: Peer framing — not a tech company selling to bettors, but bettors building for themselves.*

## App meta — Product tagline
"Your AI betting buddy for NBA props. Get L5/L10 stats + context in seconds."
*Why it's representative: Uses "buddy" (peer framing), betting jargon the audience knows (L5/L10), and promises speed — all in one sentence.*

## Landing page — CTA
"Try BetBro Free"
*Why it's representative: Three words, no pressure, no exclamation mark. Confident enough to not need hype.*
